description_long: This object is a roughly cylindrical stone block with an irregular, natural surface texture and a predominantly brown coloration. The top face features a slightly flattened area with some protruding, lighter-toned stone, possibly indicating a natural inclusion or weathered deposit. The sides are uneven, with subtle indentations and patches of lighter and darker hues, suggesting exposure to the elements. The bottom and rear faces are more uniformly rounded and lack significant distinguishing features. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. Its overall appearance suggests it could serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban or landscaped environment.
description: A dense, roughly cylindrical brown stone block with a natural, weathered surface and some lighter inclusions on the top face.
description_view_0: The stone block is seen from a side angle, showing its irregular, rounded surface and a glimpse of the lighter-toned top face.
description_view_1: This view highlights the top face with its lighter protrusions and more pronounced surface features, making it the most visually distinct side.
description_view_2: The block is viewed from another side, showing a smoother, more uniform surface with fewer distinguishing marks.
description_view_3: The rear view displays the most featureless and rounded side, with a consistent brown coloration and minimal surface variation.
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set directly on compacted ground
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows signs of natural weathering with some lighter patches and minor surface erosion, especially on the top face.
